pointSystem.q: How does the point system work?
pointSystem.a: You earn points by predicting results: a correct winner scores 3 pts; the exact score adds 2 more. A perfect prediction is worth 5 pts in total.

winner.q: What does picking the winner mean?
winner.a: It means correctly predicting who wins (home, away, or draw). If you pick 2-1 and the actual result is 3-0, you still nailed the winner (home) and score 3 pts.

exact.a: Nailing the exact goals for each team. If you pick 2-1 and the result is exactly 2-1, you score 5 pts (3 for the winner + 2 for the exact).

champion.a: In some tournaments you can pick the team you think will win it all. Get it right and you score 15 bonus pts. You can change your pick until the deadline.

topScorer.q: What is the top scorer prediction?
topScorer.a: You pick the player you think will be the tournament's top scorer. If you nail it, you get 15 bonus pts. You can change your pick until the deadline.

deadline.q: When can I make my predictions?
deadline.a: You can make or change predictions up until kickoff. Once the match starts, your pick is locked.

changePick.a: Yes, change it as many times as you want before kickoff. Only the last prediction counts.

privateGroups.a: Private groups let you compete with friends on a separate leaderboard. Create a group and share the invite code. Each group has its own ranking, but uses the same tournament points.

noPrediction.q: What happens if I don't predict a match?
noPrediction.a: If you don't predict a match, you just score 0 for that match. No penalty.

etPenalties.q: What about extra time and penalties?
etPenalties.a: Extra time goals count normally. If the match goes to penalties, it counts as a draw — penalties only decide who advances, not the scoreline.

tieBreak.q: How are ties broken between players with the same points?
tieBreak.a: When multiple players are tied on points, the ranking breaks ties in this order: 1) most correct winners, 2) most exact scores, 3) highest sum of goals in exact scores, 4) earliest first prediction.
tieBreak.exampleNote: Ana and Pablo have the same points and exact scores. Ana ranks first because her sum of goals in exact scores is higher (3 vs 1).
